{{神社| 名称=山内神社| 所在地=高知県高知市鷹匠町2-4-6| 祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主| 社格=別格官幣社| 創建=昭和9年（1935年）| 例祭=[[11月10日]]}}
'''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。旧[[社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。
==歴史== 明治4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代知藩事となった[[山内豊範]]が祖先の霊を祀るため、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に社殿を造営し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀ったのに始まる。
昭和7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内豊信|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、2人を除く初代以降歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座して、跡地に新しい社殿の造営工事を行った。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に落成し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る神社となった。平成2年、[[高知城]]内にあった熊野・春目・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。
==外部リンク== *[http://www8.ocn.ne.jp/~y-jinjya/ 山内神社]（公式サイト）
[[Category:高知県の神社|やまうちしんしや]] [[Category:別格官幣社|やまうちしんしや]] [[Category:単立神社|やまうちしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=山内神社| 所在地=高知県高知市鷹匠町2-4-6| 祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主| 社格=別格官幣社| 創建=昭和9年（1935年）| 例祭=[[11月10日]]}}
'''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。旧[[社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。
==歴史== [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の一豊とその夫人の見性院（千代）、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。天保6年（1835年）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。
明治4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代知藩事となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に社殿を造営し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。
昭和7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内豊信|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、藤並神社御旅所に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座して、跡地に新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代の全ての藩主を祀る神社となった。平成2年、高知城内にあった熊野・春目・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。

昭和7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内豊信|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、藤並神社御旅所に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座して、跡地に新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代の全ての藩主を祀る神社となった。平成2年、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。

{{神社| 名称=山内神社| 画像=[[画像:Yamauchi-jinja04o3872.jpg|280px]]| 所在地=高知県高知市鷹匠町2-4-65| 祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 | 社格=別格官幣社| 創建=昭和9年（1935年）| 例祭=[[11月10日]]}} [[画像:Yamauchi-jinja02s3872.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]]
'''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。旧[[社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]等に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。
==歴史== [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の一豊とその夫人の見性院（千代）、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。天保6年（1835年）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。
明治4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代知藩事となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に社殿を造営し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。
昭和7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内豊信|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、藤並神社御旅所に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座して、跡地に新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代の全ての藩主を祀る神社となった。平成2年、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。

{{神社| 名称=山内神社| 画像=[[画像:Yamauchi-jinja04o3872.jpg|280px]]| 所在地=高知県高知市鷹匠町2-4-65| 祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 | 社格=別格官幣社| 創建=昭和9年（1935年）| 例祭=[[11月10日]]}} [[画像:Yamauchi-jinja02s3872.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]]
'''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。旧[[社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]等に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。
==歴史== [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の一豊とその夫人の見性院（千代）、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。天保6年（1835年）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。
明治4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代知藩事となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に社殿を造営し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。
昭和7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内豊信|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、藤並神社御旅所に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座して、跡地に新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代の全ての藩主を祀る神社となった。平成2年、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。

revision.comment: プロジェクト：神道の合意による
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=山内神社| 画像=[[画像:Yamauchi-jinja04o3872.jpg|280px]]| 所在地=高知県高知市鷹匠町2-4-65| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.93|N|133|31|54.58|E|}}| 祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 | 社格=別格官幣社| 創建=昭和9年（1935年）| 例祭=[[11月10日]]}} [[画像:Yamauchi-jinja02s3872.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]]
'''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]等に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。
== 歴史 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の一豊とその夫人の見性院（千代）、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。
[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代知藩事となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に山内神社を創建し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。翌明治5年（[[1872年]]）、山内神社は高知県庁に引き渡され、村社に列格した。
[[昭和]]7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内豊信|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、山内神社に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座し、新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代の全ての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。
== 周辺 == *[[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（隣接） *[[土佐山内家宝物資料館]]（隣接） *[[鏡川]]（隣接） *[[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（国の重要文化財） *[[高知市立龍馬の生まれた町記念館]]
== 関連項目 == *[[土佐山内氏]]
== 参考文献 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、243頁
== 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Yamauchi-jinja}} *[http://www8.ocn.ne.jp/~y-jinjya/ 山内神社]（公式サイト）

== 歴史 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の一豊とその夫人の見性院（千代）、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。

昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代の全ての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。

{{神社| 名称=山内神社| 画像=[[画像:Yamauchi-jinja04o3872.jpg|280px]]| 所在地=高知県高知市鷹匠町2-4-65| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.93|N|133|31|54.58|E|region:JP-39_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=山内神社}}| 祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 | 社格=別格官幣社| 創建=昭和9年（1935年）| 例祭=[[11月10日]]}} [[画像:Yamauchi-jinja02s3872.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]]
'''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]等に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。
== 歴史 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の一豊とその夫人の見性院（千代）、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。
[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代知藩事となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に山内神社を創建し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。翌明治5年（[[1872年]]）、山内神社は[[高知県庁]]に引き渡され、村社に列格した。
[[昭和]]7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内豊信|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、山内神社に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座し、新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代の全ての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。

{{神社 |名称=山内神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja04o3872.jpg|280px|山内神社]] |所在地=高知県高知市鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.93|N|133|31|54.58|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=別格官幣社 |創建=昭和9年（1935年） 例祭=[[11月10日]] }} {{mapplot|133.531828|33.555536|山内神社}} [[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja02s3872.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]] '''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]等に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。
== 歴史 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の一豊とその夫人の見性院（千代）、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。
[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代知藩事となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に山内神社を創建し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。翌明治5年（[[1872年]]）、山内神社は[[高知県庁]]に引き渡され、村社に列格した。
[[昭和]]7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内豊信|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、山内神社に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座し、新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代のすべての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。
== 周辺 == * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（隣接） * [[土佐山内家宝物資料館]]（隣接） * [[鏡川]]（隣接） * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（国の重要文化財） * [[高知市立龍馬の生まれた町記念館]]
== 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、243頁
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[土佐山内氏]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://yamauchijinja.net/ 山内神社]（公式サイト）

昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代のすべての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。

{{神社 |名称=山内神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja04o3872.jpg|280px|山内神社]] |所在地=高知県高知市鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.93|N|133|31|54.58|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=別格官幣社 |創建=昭和9年（1935年） 例祭=[[11月10日]] }} {{mapplot|133.531828|33.555536|山内神社}} [[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja02s3872.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]] '''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]等に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。
== 歴史 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の一豊とその夫人の見性院（千代）、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。
[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代知藩事となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に山内神社を創建し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。翌明治5年（[[1872年]]）、山内神社は[[高知県庁]]に引き渡され、村社に列格した。
[[昭和]]7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内容堂|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、山内神社に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座し、新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代のすべての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。

{{神社 |名称=山内神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja04o3872.jpg|280px|山内神社]] |所在地=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.93|N|133|31|54.58|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]9年（[[1935年]]） |例祭=[[11月10日]] }} {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.555536|133.531828}}|caption=山内神社|width=256}} [[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja02s3872.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]] '''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]などに属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。
== 歴史 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の[[山内一豊|一豊]]とその夫人の[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|見性院（千代）]]、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。
[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代[[藩知事]]となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に山内神社を創建し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。翌明治5年（[[1872年]]）、山内神社は[[高知県庁]]に引き渡され、村社に列格した。
[[昭和]]7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内容堂|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、山内神社に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座し、新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代のすべての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。
== 周辺 == * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（隣接） * [[土佐山内家宝物資料館]]（隣接） * [[鏡川]]（隣接） * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（国の重要文化財） * [[高知市立龍馬の生まれた町記念館]]
== 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、243頁
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[土佐山内氏]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://yamauchijinjya.net/ 山内神社]（公式サイト）

{{神社 |名称=山内神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja04o3872.jpg|280px|山内神社]] |所在地=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.93|N|133|31|54.58|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]9年（[[1935年]]） |例祭=[[11月10日]] }} {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|33.555536|133.531828}}|caption=山内神社|width=256}} [[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja inside the complex.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]] '''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]などに属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。
== 歴史 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（[[1806年]]）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の[[山内一豊|一豊]]とその夫人の[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|見性院（千代）]]、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。
[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代[[藩知事]]となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に山内神社を創建し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。翌明治5年（[[1872年]]）、山内神社は[[高知県庁]]に引き渡され、村社に列格した。
[[昭和]]7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内容堂|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、山内神社に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座し、新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。
昭和20年（[[1945年]]）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（[[1970年]]）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代のすべての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。
== 周辺 == * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（隣接） * [[土佐山内家宝物資料館]]（隣接） * [[鏡川]]（隣接） * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（国の重要文化財） * [[高知市立龍馬の生まれた町記念館]]
== 関連図書 == *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂 山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』104〜107頁 二六興信所 1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、243頁
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[土佐山内氏]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://yamauchijinjya.net/ 山内神社]（公式サイト）
{{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}}

[[昭和]]7年（[[1933年]]）、15代[[山内容堂|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、山内神社に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座し、新しい社殿を造営した。昭和9年（[[1935年]]）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported: importing all the Bekkaku Kanpeisha from simple english wikipedia which covers all of them

revision.text:
{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Yamauchi Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|frameless]] |}
{{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref> It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku kanpeisha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]]

{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color:" |Yamauchi Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|frameless]] |}
{{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref> It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]]
== History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.
In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine.
In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.
In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref> It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]]
== History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.
In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine.
In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.
In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]]

In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref> It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]]
== History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.
In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine.
In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.
In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]

The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.
In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{神社 |名称=山内神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |例祭=[[11月10日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamauchi Shrine}}[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Bekkaku-sha]]

拝殿 |所在地=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |例祭=[[11月10日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamauchi Shrine}}[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Bekkaku-sha]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamauchi Shrine}}[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Bekkaku-sha]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yamauchi Shrine}][[Category:qq]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]][[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]][[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{神社 |名称=山内神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |例祭=[[11月10日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamauchi Shrine}}[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Bekkaku-sha]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yamauchi Shrine}][[Category:qq]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

拝殿 |所在地=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |例祭=[[11月10日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamauchi Shrine}}[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Bekkaku-sha]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yamauchi Shrine}][[Category:qq]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]][[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]][[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] ||name=Yamauchi Shrine|native_name=山内神社 |image=[[File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References ==
