{{神社 |名称=山内神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |例祭=[[11月10日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja inside the complex.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]] '''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]などに属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 == 歴史 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（1806年）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の[[山内一豊|一豊]]とその夫人の[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|見性院（千代）]]、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。[[天保]]6年（1835年）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。 [[明治]]4年（1871年）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代[[藩知事]]となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に山内神社を創建し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。翌明治5年（1872年）、山内神社は[[高知県庁]]に引き渡され、村社に列格した。 [[昭和]]7年（1933年）、15代[[山内容堂|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、山内神社に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座し、新しい社殿を造営した。昭和10年（1935年）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。 昭和20年（1945年）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（1970年）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代のすべての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（1990年）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。 == 周辺 == * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（隣接） * [[土佐山内家宝物資料館]]（隣接） * [[鏡川]]（隣接） * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（国の重要文化財） * [[高知市立龍馬の生まれた町記念館]] == 関連図書 == *{{Cite book|和書 |title=勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史 |year=1935 |publisher=二六興信所 |pages=104-107 |editor=二六興信所編纂　山田米吉 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/63}} *{{Cite book|和書 |title=容堂公と政権返上の建白 |year=1940 |publisher=山内神社 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1143264 |author=井野辺茂雄 |author-link=井野辺茂雄}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=神社辞典 |year=1979 |publisher=[[東京堂出版]] |page=243 |editor=[[白井永二]]・[[土岐昌訓]] |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/12266197}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[土佐山内氏]] == 外部リンク == * [http://yamauchijinjya.net/ 山内神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまうちしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:高知市の歴史]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:土佐藩]] [[Category:山内一豊]] 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]][[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]][[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] ||name=Yamauchi Shrine|native_name=山内神社 |image=[[File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] ||name=Yamauchi Shrine|native_name=山内神社 |image=[[File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]][[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] ||name=Yamauchi Shrine|native_name=山内神社 |image=[[File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871年}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872年}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835年}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933年}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935年}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945年}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970年}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990年}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10th year （[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835th year }}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933rd year }}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935th year }}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945th year }}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970th year }}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990th year }}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10th year （[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835th year }}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933rd year }}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935th year }}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945th year }}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970th year }}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990th year }}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10th year （[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]][[ja:山内神社]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835th year }}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933rd year }}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935th year }}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945th year }}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970th year }}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990th year }}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == 拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10th year （[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年|12=draft}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=draft}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城|12=draft}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊|12=draft}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=draft}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=draft}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835th year|12=draft}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=draft}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=draft}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933rd year|12=draft}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂|12=draft}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935th year|12=draft}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970th year|12=draft}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|12=draft}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} {{citation needed|Yamauchi Shrine}}[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Yamauchi Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[Category:Bekkaku-sha]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[ja:山内神社]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[sn:amauchi Shrine]] [[sn:Yamauchi Shrine]]

In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835th year|12=draft}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933rd year|12=draft}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂|12=draft}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935th year|12=draft}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970th year|12=draft}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|12=draft}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} {{citation needed|Yamauchi Shrine}}[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Yamauchi Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[Category:Bekkaku-sha]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[ja:山内神社]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[sn:amauchi Shrine]] [[sn:Yamauchi Shrine]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] ||name=Yamauchi Shrine|native_name=山内神社 |image=[[File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10th year （[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年|12=draft}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=draft}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城|12=draft}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊|12=draft}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=draft}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=draft}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835th year|12=draft}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=draft}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=draft}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933rd year|12=draft}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂|12=draft}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935th year|12=draft}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970th year|12=draft}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|12=draft}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} {{citation needed|Yamauchi Shrine}}[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Yamauchi Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[Category:Bekkaku-sha]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[ja:山内神社]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[sn:amauchi Shrine]] [[sn:Yamauchi Shrine]]

The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Yamauchi Shrine|Yamauchi Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] ||name=Yamauchi Shrine|native_name=山内神社 |image=[[File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10th year （[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社}} (山内神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]]..{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}} It used to be a [[Beppyo shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013. [[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] == History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|ja|1806年|12=draft}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=draft}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|ja|高知城|12=draft}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|ja|山内一豊|12=draft}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=draft}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=draft}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[Tenpō|Tenpō]] ({{ill|1835|ja|1835th year|12=draft}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed. In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|12=draft}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Toyonori Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=draft}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=draft}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and ranked as a village shrine. In the 7th year of [[Shōwa|Shōwa]] ({{ill|1933|ja|1933rd year|12=draft}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Toyonobu Yamanouchi|ja|山内容堂|12=draft}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|ja|1935th year|12=draft}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori. In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|ja|1970th year|12=draft}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|12=draft}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} {{citation needed|Yamauchi Shrine}}[[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Yamauchi Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo_shrines_without_templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[Category:Bekkaku-sha]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamauchi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[ja:山内神社]] [[en:Yamauchi Shrine]] [[sn:amauchi Shrine]] [[sn:Yamauchi Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=山内神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg|250px|山内神社]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10年（[[1935年]]） |例祭=[[11月10日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:Yamauchi-jinja inside the complex.jpg|thumb|240px|社務所、西神門]] '''山内神社'''（やまうちじんじゃ）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[土佐藩]]初代藩主[[山内一豊]]、[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|同夫人]]、および土佐藩歴代藩主を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]などに属さない[[単立|単立神社]]となっている。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 == 歴史 == [[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（1806年）、土佐藩10代藩主[[山内豊策|豊策]]により、初代藩主の[[山内一豊|一豊]]とその夫人の[[見性院 (山内一豊室)|見性院（千代）]]、2代藩主[[山内忠義|忠義]]を祀るために[[高知城]]内に造営された'''藤並神社'''に始まる。当時、初代藩主を神として祀ることが各藩で行われていた。[[天保]]6年（1835年）、「藤並大明神」の神号が贈られた。 [[明治]]4年（1871年）、土佐藩最後（16代）の藩主で、初代[[藩知事]]となった[[山内豊範|豊範]]が、藤並神社の[[御旅所]]であった現在地に山内神社を創建し、3代[[山内忠豊|忠豊]]から14代[[山内豊惇|豊惇]]までの霊を祀った。翌明治5年（1872年）、山内神社は[[高知県庁]]に引き渡され、村社に列格した。 [[昭和]]7年（1933年）、15代[[山内容堂|豊信]]（容堂）・16代豊範の[[明治維新]]での功績を顕彰する神社を新たに造ることとなり、山内神社に祀られていた歴代藩主の霊を藤並神社に遷座し、新しい社殿を造営した。昭和10年（1935年）4月に竣工し、豊信・豊範を祀る「別格官幣社山内神社」として創建された。 昭和20年（1945年）、戦火により両神社とも焼失し、以降、仮宮で祭祀が行われていた。昭和45年（1970年）、社殿を再建して藤並神社を合祀し、土佐藩歴代のすべての藩主を祀る神社となった。[[平成]]2年（1990年）、高知城内にあった熊野・春日・住吉の三社を境内に遷座した。 == 周辺 == * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（隣接） * [[土佐山内家宝物資料館]]（隣接） * [[鏡川]]（隣接） * [[旧山内家下屋敷長屋]]（国の重要文化財） * [[高知市立龍馬の生まれた町記念館]] == 関連図書 == *{{Cite book|和書 |title=勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史 |year=1935 |publisher=二六興信所 |pages=104-107 |editor=二六興信所編纂　山田米吉 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/63}} *{{Cite book|和書 |title=容堂公と政権返上の建白 |year=1940 |publisher=山内神社 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1143264 |author=井野辺茂雄 |author-link=井野辺茂雄}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=神社辞典 |year=1979 |publisher=[[東京堂出版]] |page=243 |editor=[[白井永二]]・[[土岐昌訓]] |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/12266197}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[土佐山内氏]] == 外部リンク == * [http://yamauchijinjya.net/ 山内神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまうちしんしや}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:高知市の歴史]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:土佐藩]] [[Category:山内一豊]] [[Category:見性院 (山内一豊室)]] [[Category:山内土佐守家|社やまうち]] [[Category:高知市の建築物]]
