In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ([[:en:1806|1806]]), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within [[:en:Kōchi Castle|Kōchi Castle]] to enshrine the first lord of the domain, [[:en:Yamauchi Kazutoyo|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi]], his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] ([[:en:1835|1835]]), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.

In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, [[:en:Yamauchi Toyonori|Toyonori Yamanouchi]], established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadatoyo Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ([[:en:1872|1872]]), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.

In the 7th year of [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1933|1933]]), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord [[:en:Yamauchi Toyoshige|Toyonobu Yamanouchi]] (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1935|1935]]) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.

In the 20th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1945|1945]]), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1970|1970]]), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ([[:en:1990|1990]]), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]]..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref>  It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.

In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ([[:en:1806|1806]]), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within [[:en:Kōchi Castle|Kōchi Castle]] to enshrine the first lord of the domain, [[:en:Yamauchi Kazutoyo|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi]], his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. In the 7th year of [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1933|1933]]), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord [[:en:Yamauchi Toyoshige|Toyonobu Yamanouchi]] (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1935|1935]]) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.

In the 20th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1945|1945]]), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1970|1970]]), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ([[:en:1990|1990]]), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]]..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref>  It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.

== History ==
In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ([[:en:1806|1806]]), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within [[:en:Kōchi Castle|Kōchi Castle]] to enshrine the first lord of the domain, [[:en:Yamauchi Kazutoyo|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi]], his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] ([[:en:1835|1835]]), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.

In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, [[:en:Yamauchi Toyonori|Toyonori Yamanouchi]], established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadatoyo Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ([[:en:1872|1872]]), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.

In the 7th year of [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1933|1933]]), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord [[:en:Yamauchi Toyoshige|Toyonobu Yamanouchi]] (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1935|1935]]) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.

In the 20th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1945|1945]]), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1970|1970]]), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ([[:en:1990|1990]]), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]]..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref>  It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.

== History ==
In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ([[:en:1806|1806]]), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within [[:en:Kōchi Castle|Kōchi Castle]] to enshrine the first lord of the domain, [[:en:Yamauchi Kazutoyo|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi]], his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] ([[:en:1835|1835]]), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.

In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, [[:en:Yamauchi Toyonori|Toyonori Yamanouchi]], established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadatoyo Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ([[:en:1872|1872]]), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.

In the 7th year of [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1933|1933]]), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord [[:en:Yamauchi Toyoshige|Toyonobu Yamanouchi]] (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1935|1935]]) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.

In the 20th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1945|1945]]), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1970|1970]]), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ([[:en:1990|1990]]), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.

== History ==
In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ([[:en:1806|1806]]), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within [[:en:Kōchi Castle|Kōchi Castle]] to enshrine the first lord of the domain, [[:en:Yamauchi Kazutoyo|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi]], his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] ([[:en:1835|1835]]), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.

In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, [[:en:Yamauchi Toyonori|Toyonori Yamanouchi]], established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadatoyo Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ([[:en:1872|1872]]), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.

In the 7th year of [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1933|1933]]), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord [[:en:Yamauchi Toyoshige|Toyonobu Yamanouchi]] (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1935|1935]]) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.

In the 20th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1945|1945]]), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1970|1970]]), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ([[:en:1990|1990]]), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]]..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref>  It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.

{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]]..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref>  It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.

== History ==
In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ([[:en:1806|1806]]), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within [[:en:Kōchi Castle|Kōchi Castle]] to enshrine the first lord of the domain, [[:en:Yamauchi Kazutoyo|Kazutoyo Yamanouchi]], his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] ([[:en:1835|1835]]), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.

In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ([[:en:1871|1871]]), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, [[:en:Yamauchi Toyonori|Toyonori Yamanouchi]], established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadatoyo Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ([[:en:1872|1872]]), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.

In the 7th year of [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1933|1933]]), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord [[:en:Yamauchi Toyoshige|Toyonobu Yamanouchi]] (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1935|1935]]) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.

In the 20th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1945|1945]]), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ([[:en:1970|1970]]), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ([[:en:1990|1990]]), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.

In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi}}..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref>  It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.

== History ==
In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|en|Kōchi Castle|WD=Q1012815|lt=Kōchi Castle}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|en|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|WD=Q1315878|lt=Kazutoyo Yamanouchi}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} ({{ill|1835|en|1835|WD=Q7597|lt=1835}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.

In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Toyonori Yamanouchi}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadatoyo Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.

In the 7th year of {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|en|Yamauchi Toyoshige|WD=Q711001|lt=Toyonobu Yamanouchi}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.

In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi}}..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref> It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.
== History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|en|Kōchi Castle|WD=Q1012815|lt=Kōchi Castle}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|en|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|WD=Q1315878|lt=Kazutoyo Yamanouchi}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} ({{ill|1835|en|1835|WD=Q7597|lt=1835}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.
In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Toyonori Yamanouchi}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Tadatoyo Yamanouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.
In the 7th year of {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|en|Yamauchi Toyoshige|WD=Q711001|lt=Toyonobu Yamanouchi}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.
In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] ||name=Yamauchi Shrine|native_name=山内神社 |image=File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10th year （[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} |prefecture={{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} |coordinates={{Coord|33.55554|N|133.53183|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref> It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.
== History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|en|Kōchi Castle|WD=Q1012815|lt=Kōchi Castle|1=Kōchi Castle}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|en|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|WD=Q1315878|lt=Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Kazutoyo}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Tadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} ({{ill|1835|en|1835|WD=Q7597|lt=1835|1=1835}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.
In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Toyonori Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyonori}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Tadatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.
In the 7th year of {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|en|Yamauchi Toyoshige|WD=Q711001|lt=Toyonobu Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyoshige}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935|1=1935}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.
In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990|1=1990}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yamauchi-jinja]][[ja:山内神社]][[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yamauchi-jinja]][[ja:山内神社]][[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yamauchi-jinja]][[ja:山内神社]][[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] ||name=Yamauchi Shrine|native_name=山内神社 |image=File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10th year （[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} |prefecture={{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} |coordinates={{Coord|33.55554|N|133.53183|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref> It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.
== History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|en|Kōchi Castle|WD=Q1012815|lt=Kōchi Castle|1=Kōchi Castle}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|en|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|WD=Q1315878|lt=Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Kazutoyo}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Tadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} ({{ill|1835|en|1835|WD=Q7597|lt=1835|1=1835}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.
In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Toyonori Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyonori}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Tadatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.
In the 7th year of {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|en|Yamauchi Toyoshige|WD=Q711001|lt=Toyonobu Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyoshige}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935|1=1935}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.
In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990|1=1990}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yamauchi-jinja]][[ja:山内神社]][[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yamauchi-jinja]][[ja:山内神社]][[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yamauchi-jinja]][[ja:山内神社]][[simple:Yamauchi Shrine]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] ||name=Yamauchi Shrine|native_name=山内神社 |image=File:Yamauchi-jinja shaden.jpg |address=[[高知県]][[高知市]]鷹匠町2-4-65 |location={{coordinates|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=土佐藩歴代藩主 |社格=旧[[別格官幣社]] |創建=[[昭和]]10th year （[[1935]]） |festival=[[11月10日]] |location map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=33|lat_sec=19.9|lon_deg=133|lon_min=31|lon_sec=54.5|label=山内神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|33|19.9|N|133|31|54.5|E}}}} |prefecture={{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} |coordinates={{Coord|33.55554|N|133.53183|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
{{ill|Yamauchi Shrine|ja|山内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Shrine|qq=}} (山内神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}..<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke S.|date=1994|title=The Petition Box in Eighteenth-Century Tosa|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/133199|journal=Journal of Japanese Studies|volume=20|issue=2|pages=423–458|doi=10.2307/133199|issn=0095-6848}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Roberts|first=Luke|date=2001-01-01|title=Translation of the Memorial of Imakita Sakubei to the Lord of Tosa, Yamauchi Toyochika|url=https://www.academia.edu/77719195/Translation_of_the_Memorial_of_Imakita_Sakubei_to_the_Lord_of_Tosa_Yamauchi_Toyochika|journal=Early Modern Japan: an interdisciplinary journal}}</ref> It used to be a [[Beppyo Shrine]], which is a shrine with a significant history and special importance. However, it left the Beppyo list in 2013.
== History == In the 3rd year of [[Bunka (era)|Bunka]] ({{ill|1806|en|1806|WD=Q6903|lt=1806|1=1806}}), under the 10th feudal lord of the Tosa Domain, {{ill|Toyosaku Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊策|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaku Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyokazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466486}}, the Fujinami Shrine was built within {{ill|Kōchi Castle|en|Kōchi Castle|WD=Q1012815|lt=Kōchi Castle|1=Kōchi Castle}} to enshrine the first lord of the domain, {{ill|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|en|Yamauchi Kazutoyo|WD=Q1315878|lt=Kazutoyo Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Kazutoyo}}, his wife {{ill|Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja|見性院（千代）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenshoin (Kazutoyo Yamanouchi's wife)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the second lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Tadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584319}}. At that time, it was common for each domain to enshrine their first lord as a deity. In the 6th year of {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} ({{ill|1835|en|1835|WD=Q7597|lt=1835|1=1835}}), the title of "Fujinami Daimyōjin" was bestowed.
In the 4th year of [[Meiji|Meiji]] ({{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}}), the last (16th) lord of the Tosa Domain and the first domain governor, {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Toyonori Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyonori}}, established the Yamanouchi Shrine at its current location, which had been the temporary shrine of the Fujinami Shrine, to enshrine the spirits of the lords from the 3rd lord {{ill|Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|ja|山内忠豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadayoshi Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Tadatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6584320}} to the 14th lord {{ill|Toyomasa Yamanouchi|ja|山内豊惇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa Yamanouchi|1=Yamanouchi Toyoatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466473}}. The following year, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}}), Yamanouchi Shrine was handed over to the Kōchi Prefecture and Ranked as a village shrine.
In the 7th year of {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), a new shrine was constructed to honor the achievements of the 15th lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|en|Yamauchi Toyoshige|WD=Q711001|lt=Toyonobu Yamanouchi|1=Yamauchi Toyoshige}} (Toyonobu) and the 16th lord Toyonori during the Meiji Restoration. The spirits of the successive lords enshrined at the Yamanouchi Shrine were moved to the Fujinami Shrine, and a new shrine building was constructed. The new shrine was completed in April of the 10th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935|1=1935}}) and was established as a special government shrine, the "Bekakkōhei-sha Yamanouchi Shrine", enshrining Toyonobu and Toyonori.
In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990|1=1990}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.
== References == {{Reflist}}

In the 20th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}}), both shrines were destroyed by fire during the war, and thereafter, rituals were held in temporary shrines. In the 45th year of Shōwa ({{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}}), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, and the Fujinami Shrine was merged, becoming a shrine that enshrines all the successive lords of the Tosa Domain. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] ({{ill|1990|en|1990|WD=Q2064|lt=1990|1=1990}}), the Kumano, Kasuga, and Sumiyoshi shrines, which had been within Kōchi Castle, were moved to the shrine grounds.
